<!-- review_stack_entry_start -->

[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/hbmartin/swift-mlx-text-to-sql/pull/3?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)

<!-- review_stack_entry_end -->
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

The changes align Swift and Python canonical result identity, including numeric edge cases, Unicode handling, deterministic serialization, and SQL extraction. Self-consistency voting now excludes empty results from agreement evidence while preserving anchor delivery. Evaluation execution separates generation and SQL errors. Artifact use, provenance, publication, and bundle inspection now perform additional integrity checks. Tests, fixtures, documentation, and reported verification counts were updated accordingly.

### Sequence Diagram(s)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant QueryPipeline
  participant SampleCandidates
  participant DigestTally
  participant DeterministicAnchor
  QueryPipeline->>SampleCandidates: execute candidate queries
  SampleCandidates->>DigestTally: submit eligible non-empty result digests
  DigestTally-->>QueryPipeline: consensus or no-consensus outcome
  QueryPipeline->>DeterministicAnchor: deliver anchor when no majority
  DeterministicAnchor-->>QueryPipeline: anchor result and status
```

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->
<!-- pre_merge_checks_walkthrough_start -->

<summary>🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 3</summary>

---




<sub>Comment `@coderabbitai help` to get the list of available commands.</sub>

<!-- tips_end -->

## Code Review

This pull request updates the Swift and Python evaluation pipelines to achieve exact parity (schema_version 2). Key changes include implementing Unicode code-point based string comparison, custom hand-rolled JSON serialization to match CPython escaping, robust handling of out-of-range doubles, excluding empty results from consensus voting, and preventing premature SQL truncation at semicolons inside string literals. Review feedback suggests optimizing performance in Swift by avoiding intermediate Array allocations during hashing and JSON encoding.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The [consumer version of Gemini Code Assist on GitHub](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-repo-code) is being sunset. Starting **June 18, 2026**, new organization installations will be blocked, and all code review activity will officially cease on **July 17, 2026**.
> For more details on the timeline and next steps, please review the [Help Documentation](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/deprecations/consumer-code-review).

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

To improve performance slightly and simplify the code, you can remove the explicit `Array` creation when appending UTF-8 data. The `Data.append(contentsOf:)` method can directly accept a `String.UTF8View` sequence.

This applies to the other `append(contentsOf: Array(...))` calls in this file as well.

```swift
        output.append(contentsOf: #"{"type":"#.utf8)
        Self.appendJSONString(kind, to: &output)
        output.append(contentsOf: #","value":"#.utf8)
        Self.appendJSONString(value.payload, to: &output)
        output.append(UInt8(ascii: "}"))

```

**Actionable comments posted: 5** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)
> >
> CREGKit/Sources/creg-eval-cli/Main.swift (1)
> > `128-147`: _🩺 Stability & Availability_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_ > > **stderr pipe is never drained — can still deadlock `waitUntilExit()`.** > > The fix drains `output` (stdout) before waiting, which correctly avoids the stdout-buffer deadlock. But `process.standardError = Pipe()` is assigned and its `fileHandleForReading` is never read. If the child (`git`, `swift --version`) writes enough to stderr to fill the OS pipe buffer, the child blocks on that write and `waitUntilExit()` hangs indefinitely — the same class of bug this change just fixed for stdout, just on the other stream. Low probability given the specific commands used here, but worth closing off since this function is explicitly hardening against exactly this kind of hang. > >
> 🔒 Proposed fix: drain both pipes > > ```diff > static func commandOutput(_ command: [String]) -> String? { > let process = Process() > let output = Pipe() > + let errorOutput = Pipe() > process.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: "/usr/bin/env") > process.arguments = command > process.standardOutput = output > - process.standardError = Pipe() > + process.standardError = errorOutput > do { > try process.run() > // Drain the pipe before waiting so output larger than the pipe > // buffer cannot deadlock the child. > let data = output.fileHandleForReading.readDataToEndOfFile() > + _ = errorOutput.fileHandleForReading.readDataToEndOfFile() > process.waitUntilExit() > guard process.terminationStatus == 0 else { return nil } > return String(decoding: data, as: UTF8.self) > .trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines) > } catch { > return nil > } > } > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the > rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. > > In `@CREGKit/Sources/creg-eval-cli/Main.swift` around lines 128 - 147, Update > commandOutput to retain the stderr Pipe and drain both stdout and stderr before > calling process.waitUntilExit(), ensuring either stream cannot block the child. > Preserve the existing successful-output trimming, termination-status check, and > nil-on-error behavior. > ``` > >
> > > >
> >
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In `@fine-tuning/eval/ex.py`: - Line 25: Update the imports in ex.py to source Iterable, Iterator, and Sequence from collections.abc instead of typing, while retaining Any from typing. In `@fine-tuning/tools/analyze_matrix.py`: - Around line 431-440: Update the identical-SQL drift check in the disagreements loop to require a non-None SQL value before comparing the Python and Swift SQL strings. Preserve the existing same-SQL and same-SQLite-version conditions for executed queries, while allowing disagreements where both predictions lack SQL to proceed normally. - Around line 421-430: Update the explanation validation flow around the explanations mapping and disagreement_ids so stale or unknown keys are checked against all raw decoded mapping keys before filtering, normalizing, or dropping non-string or blank values. Preserve the existing SelectionError behavior for stale explanation IDs while ensuring entries such as blank or null values cannot hide invalid keys. In `@fine-tuning/tools/fetch_model.py`: - Around line 612-625: Restrict the replacement check around replaceable and shutil.rmtree so a directory is deletable only when it has a dedicated materialization marker or matches the expected model bundle inventory; do not treat the presence of config.json alone as sufficient. Preserve the refusal path for unrelated or unrecognized directories and update the error wording to reflect the stronger validation. In `@fine-tuning/tools/publish_finalists.py`: - Around line 452-467: Update the unexpected-file verification near staged_paths to enumerate published paths directly with fresh.rglob("*") instead of relying on directory_inventory(fresh), while excluding only the established local download-cache metadata if applicable. Preserve the .gitattributes exception and raise the existing RuntimeError for any unstaged lock, cache, or other file. --- Outside diff comments: In `@CREGKit/Sources/creg-eval-cli/Main.swift`: - Around line 128-147: Update commandOutput to retain the stderr Pipe and drain both stdout and stderr before calling process.waitUntilExit(), ensuring either stream cannot block the child. Preserve the existing successful-output trimming, termination-status check, and nil-on-error behavior. ```
🪄 Autofix (Beta) Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR: - [ ] Push a commit to this branch (recommended) - [ ] Create a new PR with the fixes
---

Ruff flags these `typing` imports as deprecated aliases (UP035).






<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-from typing import Any, Iterable, Iterator, Sequence
+from collections.abc import Iterable, Iterator, Sequence
+from typing import Any
```
</details>

<!-- cr-comment:v1:2bb5b5c21104a849c912bf21 -->

_Source: Linters/SAST tools_

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_🗄️ Data Integrity & Integration_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Validate all explanation keys before normalizing values.**

The `explanations` map was built by dropping non-string or blank values before this check. Therefore entries such as `{"stale-id": ""}` or `{"stale-id": null}` disappear, allowing a raw explanation file with stale keys to pass when all real disagreements are explained. Validate the raw decoded mapping before computing stale IDs.






<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
+        raw_explanation_ids = {str(item_id) for item_id in decoded}
+        if any(
+            not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip()
+            for value in decoded.values()
+        ):
+            raise SelectionError(
+                "parity explanations must contain non-empty strings"
+            )
...
-    stale_explanations = sorted(set(explanations) - disagreement_ids)
+    stale_explanations = sorted(raw_explanation_ids - disagreement_ids)
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
    # Explanations are structurally validated: they may cover only actual
    # disagreements, and identical SQL diverging on the same SQLite engine
    # is comparator or runtime drift that no explanation can excuse.
    raw_explanation_ids = {str(item_id) for item_id in decoded}
    if any(
        not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip()
        for value in decoded.values()
    ):
        raise SelectionError(
            "parity explanations must contain non-empty strings"
        )
    disagreement_ids = {item["id"] for item in disagreements}
    stale_explanations = sorted(raw_explanation_ids - disagreement_ids)
    if stale_explanations:
        raise SelectionError(
            "explanations cover items that are not disagreements "
            f"(stale or wrong file): {stale_explanations}"
        )
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🧰 Tools</summary>

<details>
<summary>🪛 Ruff (0.15.21)</summary>

[warning] 427-430: Avoid specifying long messages outside the exception class

(TRY003)

</details>

</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@fine-tuning/tools/analyze_matrix.py` around lines 421 - 430, Update the
explanation validation flow around the explanations mapping and disagreement_ids
so stale or unknown keys are checked against all raw decoded mapping keys before
filtering, normalizing, or dropping non-string or blank values. Preserve the
existing SelectionError behavior for stale explanation IDs while ensuring
entries such as blank or null values cannot hide invalid keys.
```

</details>

<!-- cr-comment:v1:53de9b4a1973844b15a680be -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_🎯 Functional Correctness_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Exclude missing predictions from identical-SQL drift detection.**

When both sides have no SQL, `None == None` is true, so this branch can reject a valid explanation as SQLite drift even though no SQL executed. Require a non-`None` SQL value before applying the same-engine identical-SQL rule.






<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
         if (
-            item["python"]["sql"] == item["swift"]["sql"]
+            item["python"]["sql"] is not None
+            and item["python"]["sql"] == item["swift"]["sql"]
             and python_sqlite == swift_sqlite
         ):
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
    for item in disagreements:
        if (
            item["python"]["sql"] is not None
            and item["python"]["sql"] == item["swift"]["sql"]
            and python_sqlite == swift_sqlite
        ):
            raise SelectionError(
                f"{item['id']}: identical SQL on the same SQLite version "
                "produced different parity data; fix runtime/comparator "
                "drift instead of explaining it"
            )
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

_🗄️ Data Integrity & Integration_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Do not treat any directory containing `config.json` as disposable.**

A mistyped destination targeting an unrelated application directory with `config.json` will pass this check and be recursively deleted. Require a dedicated materialization marker or validate the existing tree against the expected model inventory before replacement.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_🗄️ Data Integrity & Integration_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Scan unfiltered paths for unexpected published files.**

`directory_inventory()` omits `.creg-artifact.json` and `.cache`, so `unexpected` cannot detect those files despite the two-way verification claim. Build the path set from `fresh.rglob("*")` (excluding only known local download-cache metadata, if applicable) so an unexpected lock or cache file in the public snapshot fails publication.

while excluding only the established local download-cache metadata if
applicable. Preserve the .gitattributes exception and raise the existing
RuntimeError for any unstaged lock, cache, or other file.
